data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260512T050728-bb18a61f/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260512T050728-bb18a61f/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260512T050728-bb18a61f/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260512T050728-bb18a61f/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260512T050728-bb18a61f/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-ef9e8aec">
    <title>spec: define forward-progress readiness override policy</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
DDx needs an explicit readiness lifecycle contract that optimizes for continuous forward progress. Today the specs both allow readiness to move ambiguous/underspecified open beads to status=proposed and say quality gates default to WARN-ONLY, leaving workers and lifecycle hooks free to park operator-accepted work repeatedly.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:219-224 defines ambiguous_requires_operator as moving the bead to status=proposed, and docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:485-493 repeats that ambiguous or underspecified work moves to proposed. It does not define operator promotion as an override.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md:85-99 says WARN-ONLY is the default, but docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md:136-173 still permits readiness to block for human input or lossy decomposition without spelling out which findings are advisory versus hard blockers.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-027-bead-collection-abstraction.md:70-75 and :785-792 define proposed -> open as triaged, but the operator-acceptance semantics are not connected to readiness re-evaluation.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:160-172 allows open -> proposed for depth overflow or ambiguity, but does not require idempotency after proposed -> open.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the governing docs to state:
- open is the forward-progress execution lane; proposed is the hard operator-decision escape hatch.
- Default pre-claim readiness is warn/improve/decompose; it must proceed for recoverable rubric gaps, lint noise, uncertain model judgment, and readiness infrastructure failures.
- Automatic open -> proposed is reserved for true operator_required outcomes, lossy decomposition, contradictory requirements, unsafe commitment loss, or explicit hard manual-decision blockers.
- proposed -> open via operator action is durable acceptance. Re-readiness must not re-park the same bead for the same rule/finding unless prompt-relevant bead fields changed or the operator explicitly requests re-triage.
- Reuse the TD-027 triaged event for proposed -> open. If new warning event kinds are needed, amend TD-027 event vocabulary in the same spec change; otherwise use existing event kinds with structured fields.

NON-SCOPE
- No Go implementation in this bead.
- No change to Fizeau routing/profile/model/provider behavior.
- No weakening of post-attempt review/manual-required outcomes that already have concrete execution evidence.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: ddx-cc0641a7. No deps.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md states that open is the forward-progress lane and that default readiness warnings proceed unless a hard operator-required condition is present.
2. docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md distinguishes warn/proceed findings from hard parking findings and names operator promotion as a durable override.
3. docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-027-bead-collection-abstraction.md and docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md define proposed -> open / triaged as the operator-acceptance signal used by readiness idempotency.
4. docs/triage/decomposition.md states how lossy/depth-cap decomposition interacts with operator acceptance without creating downgrade loops.
5. Documentation grep check passes: `rg -n "operator acceptance|triaged|WARN-ONLY|open is the forward-progress" docs/helix docs/triage` shows the new policy language in the expected files.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
Operator reviewed readiness downgrade on 2026-05-12. Bead is execution-ready as written; readiness parked it because its attempted rewrite dropped the explicit NON-SCOPE commitment about not weakening post-attempt review/manual-required outcomes. Add triage:skip so this bead can proceed and fix the downgrade-loop policy.
    </notes>
    <labels>phase:2, area:docs, area:agent, area:bead-lifecycle, kind:docs, prevention, spec:FEAT-010, adr:023, td:027, td:031, triage:skip</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-cc0641a7" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="269f4c43de8e2e55337b5a1c665365880f1dd5d5" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260512T050728-bb18a61f"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Blocked on verification: `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` did not pass, so I did not commit.

- I updated the policy docs in [FEAT-010](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ef9e8aec-20260512T050728-bb18a61f/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md), [ADR-023](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ef9e8aec-20260512T050728-bb18a61f/docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md), [TD-027](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ef9e8aec-20260512T050728-bb18a61f/docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-027-bead-collection-abstraction.md), [TD-031](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ef9e8aec-20260512T050728-bb18a61f/docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md), and [docs/triage/decomposition.md](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ef9e8aec-20260512T050728-bb18a61f/docs/triage/decomposition.md) to spell out the forward-progress lane, `triaged` as durable operator acceptance, and the no-downgrade-loop rule.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passed.
- The Go gate failed on existing test issues: `TestConcurrentLand_PreservationRateUnderLoadIsBounded` timed out and `TestHeartbeatKeepsActiveClaimAlive` failed.
- I recorded the blocker in [no_changes_rationale.txt](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ef9e8aec-20260512T050728-bb18a61f/.ddx/executions/20260512T050728-bb18a61f/no_changes_rationale.txt).

1. If you want, I can investigate the failing `cli/internal/agent` and `cli/internal/bead` tests next.
2. If you want to keep this bead doc-only, I can wait for the test baseline to be fixed and then finish the commit.
